zh.lifeSummary: 當Ann Carter於1554年出生在英國貝德福爾肯普斯頓時，她的父親William H. Carter Sr44歲，母親Elizabeth Catherine Cranfield40歲。她去世於1605年12月1日在她的家鄉享年51歲。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Ann Carter was born in 1554, in Kempston, Bedfordshire, England, her father, William H. Carter Sr, was 44 and her mother, Elizabeth Catherine Cranfield, was 40. She died on 1 December 1605, in her hometown, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ann Carter est née en 1554, à Kempston, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, William H. Carter Sr, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Catherine Cranfield, avait 40 ans. Elle est décédée le 1 décembre 1605, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ann Carter 1554 in Kempston, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William H. Carter Sr 44 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Catherine Cranfield 40. Sie starb am 1. Dezember 1605 im Alter von 51 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ann Carter è nata il nel 1554 nel Kempston, Bedfordshire, England, sua padre, William H. Carter Sr, aveva 44 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Catherine Cranfield, aveva 40 anni. Morì il 1 dicembre 1605, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 51 anni.
